assay_contract.denominator_caveat: The article states normalization by PHH cell number; it does not identify a window-specific viable-cell denominator.

observed_batch_span.scope: All six Table S3 batch means plus SD values; this is not a population reference interval.

reported_associations.model_consequence: Do not infer a transcription-to-secretion rate law from this association.
Do not use albumin secretion as a numeric proxy for CYP450 activity.

quantity_audit.reason: The ELISA endpoint identifies only cumulative extracellular albumin over the 24-hour window.
Extracellular endpoint alone does not separate translation from downstream trafficking or loss.
No PHH ER-resolved pulse-chase measurement was reported.
The 24-hour medium endpoint does not resolve Golgi processing.
The endpoint conflates exocytosis with synthesis, trafficking and intracellular loss.
No matched intracellular precursor or mature albumin loss measurement was reported.

required_measurements.label: Donor-resolved albumin secretion time course
requirements: matched culture medium, matrix, oxygen and donor metadata
required_measurements.purpose: Replace endpoint-only output with donor-specific secretion trajectories and an auditable denominator.

required_measurements.label: PHH compartment-resolved pulse-chase
requirements: labelled albumin in ER, Golgi and medium fractions
enough timepoints to estimate separate transit rates
required_measurements.purpose: Identify ER export and Golgi maturation instead of borrowing hepatoma transit constants.

required_measurements.label: Matched ALB synthesis and transcript time course
required_measurements.purpose: Separate transcriptional state from translation and cumulative secretion.

required_measurements.label: Intracellular albumin pool time course
required_measurements.purpose: Constrain intracellular storage, processing and degradation terms.

required_measurements.label: Matched secretory-path perturbation time course
requirements: ER export, Golgi processing or exocytosis perturbation
required_measurements.purpose: Disentangle causal secretory-path rates without fitting all rates to one endpoint.

limitations: All six Table S3 means and SD values are loaded from the MD5-verified supplementary DOCX; the table reports n=3 but does not identify the replicate class, and the summaries are not a population interval.
The six commercial PHH batches are not an in-vivo healthy-human population reference cohort.
The assay reports a 24-hour cumulative endpoint and cannot identify translation, ER export, Golgi maturation, exocytosis or degradation rates.
The 19.33-million median copies-per-nucleus proteome anchor comes from a different seven-donor cohort and is a total protein-group context, not a secretion rate or per-hepatocyte count.
The CSCB threshold is a PHH product-quality criterion, not a physiological interval or a simulation pass threshold.
